title: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) :  obligations in France
bodyHtml:
Disclaimer: Note that Back Market doesn’t provide legal advice, and that it’s your responsibility to remain up to date with existing regulations. For additional information, contact your legal advisors.
 

What’s EPR? 

Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is an environmental policy that makes the party who first places an item on the market subject to EPR requirements. This party is also known as the “producer.” They’re responsible for the entire lifecycle of the item, from design to end-of-life disposal, including waste collection and treatment). Under EPR regulations, the producer must mitigate the environmental impacts of their items throughout the entire item lifecycle by paying a fee. 
 

How does the EPR system works in France? 

The French government has introduced a new regulation called Loi AGEC (Loi anti-gaspillage pour une économie circulaire) to promote a circular economy and reduce waste. 

Under this regulation, marketplaces such as Back Market are required to prevent and manage waste and ensure that sellers selling in the French market have a unique identification number (IDU).

There are many different categories of EPR: Batteries, Packaging, Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), and others. Each of these categories has 1 or more Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) who’s responsible for registering the producers, providing them with a unique IDU number and collecting fees. 

Sellers are responsible for registering with the relevant Producer Responsibility Organization. 
 

Am I a producer under French regulations? 

A producer isn’t the same as a manufacturer. The producer is the one who first puts an item for sale in France. 
 

What items are subject to EPR? 

Several categories of items sold on Back Market are concerned. 

The main ones are:  

  • Packaging 
  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) 
  • Batteries 
  • Paper for user manuals (note that you may need to register even if you’re below the 5-ton limit)

With some exceptions, you must get a separate IDU number for each category.

For example, if you’re selling phones and tablets, you need to register with 3 different Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs): 1 for Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), 1 for Batteries, and 1 for Packaging. This means you’ll get 3 different IDU numbers. 

Here are the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs) associated with each EPR category: 

EPR CategoryProducer Responsibility Organizations (PROs)
Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)https://www.ecosystem.eco
https://www.ecologic-france.com
Batterieshttps://www.corepile.fr/
https://www.screlec.fr/
Packaginghttps://www.citeo.com
https://www.leko-organisme.fr
Paperhttps://www.citeo.com


Depending on the type of items you sell, you may need to contact other Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs) who can handle other categories of items. 
 

How do I register? 

Note that these steps apply only to France. You’ll need to register separately for other markets such as Germany or Austria. 

To register, here’s that you need to do: 

  1. Contact a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) for each category
  2. Get a registration number for each category, and in some cases, pay fees that can vary from 0 € to several hundred euros 
  3. After you’re registered with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO), they’ll contact the ADEME and give you your IDU number
  4. Add your IDU numbers to your Back Office > “Business profile” > “Legal details” 

If you need assistance, some service providers offer support services like Avask, or Take-e-way  

Note that these providers are independent of Back Market. 
 

Is registration mandatory in France?

Yes. If you’re not registered and still want to sell in France, Back Market will pay the fees on your behalf and will charge you. This will be more expensive than registering for EPR on your own. Back Market isn’t able to benefit from reduced EPR fees when declaring on your behalf.
 

What do I need to do after registering? 

Once registered, you must: 

  • Add your IDU number for each category depending on the type of items you sell. 
  • Report the quantity of items that you sold in France to the relevant Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs). 
  • Pay fees to the Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs). 

The fees are calculated by each Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) according to sales, categories of items, and the volume of items put on the French market. 
 

What numbers should I add to my Back Office? 

You’ll have to give Back Market your EPR number for each category. Please note that if you have several accounts you must fill in your EPR number for each account. 

Back Market will verify your numbers once submitted in your Back Office.

The format of the numbers no matter the category is FRXXXXXX_XXXXXX. 

Please note that your Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) registration number isn’t the same as your IDU number for each category. You need to add your unique IDU number for each category in your Back Office > “Your business profile” > “Legal details”



Click “Add details” 



Choose your category and add the corresponding IDU number. 

I’m buying items from an upstream supplier. What do I need to do? 

If your upstream supplier is located in France and is registered with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO), your supplier is required to share their IDU number with you. You’re required to add your supplier’s IDU number to your Back Office. If you have more than 1 supplier, you’ll need to provide all IDU numbers.  

If your upstream supplier isn’t located in France, you’re considered to be the producer for EPR purposes, and you’re required to register with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) and add your IDU number to your Back Office. 

If both of these situations apply to you, you need to add the following to your Back Office: 

  • The IDU number of your upstream supplier located in France
  • Your own IDU number 


What if my supplier doesn’t give me their EPR number? 

If your upstream supplier doesn’t want to give you their EPR registration number, you must register with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) and provide your EPR registration number to Back Market. Back Market will pay the EPR fees to the relevant Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) on your behalf and will invoice the fees to you. 
 

What happens if I don’t add my IDU number to my Back Office? 

Back Market will pay the EPR fees to the relevant Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) on your behalf and will invoice the fees to you.
Back Market will charge you quarterly installments based on your sales in France over the previous quarter.

Since February 2024, Back Market is also charging a non-refundable 50 € admin fee per quarter. If you declare and pay the fees directly to the agency and have provided valid IDU numbers in your Back Office or you’re exempt from EPR fees, you won’t be charged.

If you’re registered with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) for only certain categories, Back Market will only invoice the fees for the categories where you’re not already registered. 

If you register with a Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) and provide Back Market with your EPR number, Back Market will stop charging you EPR fees on the next quarterly installment.

Here are example fees for the following 3 categories, applicable starting 1st July 2024, provided by Producer Responsibility Organizations (PROs): 

EPR SECTOR SMARTPHONESLAPTOPS TABLETS HEADPHONES
WEEE3.34 cents0.63 cents0.63 cents 0.20 cents
Batteries 0.30 cents 0.30 cents0.30 cents 0.30 cents 
Packaging0.1461 cents 0.1540 cents 0.1540 cents 0.1592 cents 

 

I only sell refurbished items. Am I exempt from EPR in France? 

Certain refurbished items can be exempt from Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) in France, notably if they were already put on the market in France and were already subject to the eco-fee in France. If you only sell refurbished items that meet these conditions, you’re not required to register for the Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) category. Contact the Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) to determine if this exemption applies to you.

If you purchase your products outside of France (whether secondhand or new), you’re subject to the EPR fee for the WEEE category as you sell products on the French market. 

If you’re exempt from Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE), you must certify this by checking the “I’m exempt from WEEE” box in your Back Office. If you’re exempt, Back Market will not charge you the Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) fees. 



However, you’re still required to register for the Batteries and Packaging categories and add your IDU numbers for these categories in your Back Office.

I want more information about EPR requirements in France. Where can I find this? 

If you want information about a particular EPR category, you can check the Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) website. 

You can also reach out to Taxually, Avask or Take-e-way if you need more assistance or information about EPR categories. Please note that these providers are independent of Back Market. 

Can Back Market share my EPR data? 

Back Market must establish and uphold a record that contains details about sellers who are selling in France. This includes identification details, VAT numbers, EPR IDU numbers, the quantity of items covered by EPR schemes by category. This register can be shared to the authorities upon request.
